objectives: Deep audit of test coverage across all 7 crates after newtype-construction-validation (items 221-226)
Identify new gaps introduced by pattern_codegen, emit/validate, and TryFrom generation
Evaluate quality of existing tests (smoke vs. meaningful assertions)
Surface property-based and fuzz testing opportunities
Assess integration test coverage for cross-crate interactions
constraints: Covers all 7 crates: codegen, model, parse, validate, translate, cli, mx20022 umbrella
important_facts: Test count has grown since prior audit: codegen inline tests now include pattern_codegen (22 pattern tests + 5 structure tests + 5 parser unit tests) and emit/validate tests (16 tests) and emit/newtypes tests (11 tests)
TryFrom<String> + new() are now generated for all constrained newtypes in all 13 schemas — but ZERO tests exercise TryFrom/new() on actual generated model types
The pattern_codegen compiler has inline tests for Rust emission validity but NO semantic tests: does the emitted code actually ACCEPT valid values and REJECT invalid ones?
schemes.rs now has 30 tests (up from ~20) including typed validation path tests for FedNow, SEPA, CBPR+
validate/tests/schemes.rs has vec_index_in_validation_paths and typed_validate_constraints_integration tests (new)
pacs.002 (3 versions: 001.10, 001.12, 001.14) still has ZERO roundtrip or validation tests
head.001.001.02 still has no tests at all
pacs.008 versions 001.08 and 001.10 still have no tests
Codegen snapshot testing still covers only head.001.001.04 — 12 other schemas have no snapshots
Translation testdata fixtures (testdata/translation/) still have only input.txt, NO expected.xml output files
parse/tests/fixtures/ and codegen/tests/fixtures/xsd/multi_file/ still contain only .gitkeep placeholder files
Coverage CI job still commented out
No proptest/quickcheck anywhere in workspace dev-dependencies
No cargo-fuzz targets anywhere in workspace
MT940 roundtrip test is string-contains only (not structural field comparison)
amount.rs rule: zero amounts are rejected (> 0 required) — but there is no doc clarifying this as a business rule vs. a format rule
SEPA f64 amount comparison (item 140) is still not_started — float precision bug not yet fixed
Items 141-145 (Display/Error impls for ValidationResult) still not_started
The validate/src/typed.rs bridge function is tested in schemes.rs (typed_validate_bridge test)
Blanket Validatable impls for Option<T>, Vec<T>, String, bool, ChoiceWrapper<T> are tested only via the doc example (no_run) — no dedicated unit tests
decisions_made: Prioritize TryFrom/new() semantic tests above all else: the pattern compiler is untested end-to-end at runtime
Property-based testing for pattern_codegen and IBAN/BIC rules is highest ROI addition
Snapshot tests for pacs.008.001.13 and camt.053.001.11 should be next after TryFrom tests
artifacts: crates/mx20022-validate/tests/schemes.rs (841 lines, 30 tests including typed path)
testdata/ (27 fixture files — no expected.* translation outputs)
dependencies: item 140: SEPA f64 amount comparison fix (not_started) — affects SEPA_AMOUNT_MAX test correctness
item 166-169: real-world-test-corpus (not_started) — would provide authoritative validation fixtures
items 157-165: newtype-validation iteration (deferred) — TryFrom/new() testing is partially blocked on this
risks_issues: Pattern codegen generates code that is checked for Rust syntax validity (syn::parse_file) but NOT semantic correctness — a buggy pattern check could silently accept invalid values: CRITICAL gap
TryFrom<String>/new() on all 13 schemas generate code with no runtime tests — regression risk high
SEPA f64 amount comparison (item 140) could cause test failures in edge cases around EUR 999,999,999.99
Coverage CI still commented out — no objective coverage measurement
Translation testdata fixtures missing expected outputs — golden-file testing not possible
pacs.002 has 0 integration tests despite being the main payment status report message

iteration.summary: Performed a full re-audit after newtype-construction-validation iteration (items 221-226). The biggest new gap is that the pattern_codegen compiler emits code that is only validated for Rust syntactic correctness via syn::parse_file — there are no semantic tests that actually run the emitted code to verify correct accept/reject behavior. All 13 regenerated schemas now have TryFrom<String>/new() but the model crate has zero tests exercising these constructors on real generated types. The typed validation path tests in schemes.rs are a meaningful addition and exercise the full parse->validate pipeline. However, pacs.002 (3 versions), head.001.001.02, and pacs.008 (v008, v010) remain completely untested end-to-end. Property-based testing and fuzz targets are still absent.
next_actions: Add semantic tests for pattern_codegen: compile and execute generated check functions against known-valid and known-invalid values for each of the 22 patterns
Add TryFrom/new() tests in mx20022-model for real generated types: BICFIDec2014Identifier, IBAN2007Identifier, CountryCode, Max35Text at minimum
Add proptest to validate and codegen dev-deps; add property-based tests for IBAN/BIC rules and pattern_codegen
Add pacs.002 roundtrip test in parse/tests/roundtrip.rs (all 3 versions if possible)
Create expected.xml files in testdata/translation/ for golden-file diff testing
Fix MT940 roundtrip: parse the re-generated MT940 back into fields and compare structurally
Add XML malformed-input edge cases: truncated mid-element, BOM prefix, namespace collision, duplicate attributes

mentions.relevance_note: Code quality review context — may have identified pattern_codegen edge cases or dead code worth testing
